_comment: Real upstream SHA-256 + size values, populated 2026-05-05 (Phase 4 of the post-Group-C-pkg-vm follow-up sequence). 9 of 10 entries point at live, downloadable upstream URLs whose bytes have been hashed against the published binaries; the 10th entry — alpine-edge-riscv64 — keeps a placeholder zero SHA because Alpine does not yet publish RISC-V cloud images upstream (probed dl-cdn.alpinelinux.org/alpine/{edge,v3.21,v3.20}/releases/cloud/ — no riscv64 qcow2 listing). The pkg/cache.Store.Get() call rejects any blob whose computed SHA-256 does not match the manifest entry's declared SHA, so attempting to use the alpine-edge-riscv64 entry will fail loudly with an honest 'SHA-256 mismatch' error rather than silently accept unverified bytes — this is anti-bluff posture per §6.J/§6.L. Operators consuming this manifest should treat alpine-edge-riscv64 as a known upstream gap and skip it from their target lists until Alpine's edge cloud images include RISC-V. The fedora-rawhide-riscv64 URL points at Fedora 42's RISC-V cloud-base (the 'rawhide' label in the entry id is historical — Fedora has stabilized 42 by 2026-05; the URL is the current published artifact, not a rolling rawhide URL). Schema conforms to pkg/cache.Manifest (submodules/containers/pkg/cache/manifest.go). The 9 qcow2/qcow2-equivalent entries cover (alpine/debian/fedora) × (x86_64/aarch64/riscv64) for the cross-arch signing matrix + cross-OS distro matrix; the 'android-system-image-*' entries are referenced by the Phase B pkg/emulator/ refactor for cache-routed system-image fetch and by Phase 2 of the AVD-cache integration that wires --image-manifest through scripts/run-emulator-tests.sh.

images._status: upstream-unavailable — Alpine does not yet publish RISC-V cloud-init qcow2 images on dl-cdn.alpinelinux.org. Placeholder kept so the manifest still declares the slot the cross-arch signing matrix expects. The pkg/cache.Store.Get() SHA-mismatch check rejects this entry on attempted use, surfacing an honest error rather than silently accepting unverified bytes.
